Details: Two fundamentally different types of outputs: environment_uspAPIPatchAccount provides basic infrastructure pricing and data volume estimates (5 lines), while uspAPIPatchAccount delivers comprehensive T-SQL stored procedure analysis with detailed complexity metrics, syntax differences, and conversion recommendations (370+ lines). The outputs serve different purposes in the SQL-to-PySpark migration workflow.

Both outputs relate to the same uspAPIPatchAccount stored procedure migration context, but address completely different aspects. environment_uspAPIPatchAccount focuses on infrastructure costs and data volumes, while uspAPIPatchAccount provides technical analysis. Limited semantic overlap as they serve different stakeholder needs in the migration project.

Completely different structural approaches: environment_uspAPIPatchAccount uses simple key-value format for pricing/volume data, while uspAPIPatchAccount follows structured technical analysis format with numbered sections (Script Overview, Complexity Metrics, Syntax Differences, Manual Adjustments, Conversion Complexity, Optimization Techniques, apiCost). No structural alignment between the outputs.

Syntactically correct pricing and data volume information. Clear format with proper units (DBU costs $0.15-$0.75/hour, data volumes in TB/GB). Only minor issue: missing currency specification for apiCost value.

Highly detailed and syntactically correct technical analysis. Comprehensive coverage of T-SQL elements, accurate complexity scoring (75/100), proper function mappings (IIF→when/otherwise), and valid optimization recommendations. Minor formatting inconsistencies in some bullet points.
Both outputs are syntactically sound within their respective domains. High accuracy in technical details and proper formatting throughout.
